name: Semantic HTML Structure
description: Use landmark elements (header, main, nav, aside, footer, section, article) to give pages meaningful structure for assistive tech and search engines.

name: Accessible HTML Forms
description: Every input must have an associated label; use fieldset/legend for groups; rely on native constraint validation before adding JS validation.
template:
## Accessible HTML Forms ```html

We'll never share your email.

Please enter a valid email.

Shipping method
``` **Rules:** - Never use `placeholder` as the only label â€” it disappears on focus - Use `autocomplete` attributes to reduce friction - Show inline errors with `role="alert"` so screen readers announce them - Use `required`, `minlength`, `pattern` for native validation - Disable submit only after the user has attempted to submit

name: CSS Custom Properties as Design Tokens
description: Define all design values (color, spacing, typography, motion) as custom properties on :root. Reference tokens everywhere instead of hardcoding values.

name: CSS Grid Layout Patterns
description: Use CSS Grid for two-dimensional layouts â€” page structure, card grids, dashboard panels. Prefer named grid areas for readability.

name: CSS Flexbox Patterns
description: Use Flexbox for one-dimensional layouts â€” navigation bars, button groups, centering, stretch-to-fill. Know when to use Grid instead.

name: CSS Animations â€” Compositor-Only Properties
description: Animate only transform, opacity, clip-path, and filter to stay on the GPU compositor thread. Animating layout properties causes reflows and janks.

name: CSS Container Queries
description: Use container queries to make components respond to their parent container size, not the viewport. Essential for reusable components in grid/flex layouts.

name: CSS Logical Properties for i18n
description: Use logical properties (inline/block) instead of physical (left/right/top/bottom) so layouts work correctly in RTL and vertical writing modes without extra CSS.

name: HTML Meta Tags for SEO & Social
description: Every page needs a complete set of meta tags: title, description, canonical, Open Graph, Twitter Card, and viewport.
template:
## HTML Meta Tags Checklist

```html
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />

  <!-- Primary SEO -->
  <title>Page Title | Brand (50-60 chars)</title>
  <meta name="description" content="150-160 char description with target keyword." />
  <link rel="canonical" href="https://example.com/page" />

  <!-- Open Graph (Facebook, LinkedIn, Slack) -->
  <meta property="og:title" content="Page Title" />
  <meta property="og:description" content="Short description." />
  <meta property="og:image" content="https://example.com/og-image.jpg" />
  <meta property="og:url" content="https://example.com/page" />
  <meta property="og:type" content="website" />

  <!-- Twitter / X Card -->
  <meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image" />
  <meta name="twitter:title" content="Page Title" />
  <meta name="twitter:description" content="Short description." />
  <meta name="twitter:image" content="https://example.com/og-image.jpg" />

  <!-- Performance -->
  <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com" crossorigin />
</head>
```

**Checklist:**
- [ ] Title is 50-60 characters
- [ ] Description is 150-160 characters with primary keyword
- [ ] Canonical URL avoids duplicate content
- [ ] OG image is 1200Ă—630px minimum
- [ ] No duplicate meta tags across pages

name: CSS @layer for Cascade Management
description: Use @layer to explicitly control specificity order between reset, tokens, components, and utilities. Prevents specificity wars.
